perturbation.original_text: VET TECH shall have ultimate responsibility for client relationships for those clients that it elects to bill directly for COOL TECH Services and COOL TECH will respond to VET TECH's direction.
perturbation.changed_text: VET TECH shall have responsibility for client relationships for those clients that it elects to bill directly for COOL TECH Services.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'COOL TECH will respond to VET TECH's direction', it introduces ambiguity and potential conflict. While VET TECH maintains initial responsibility, the removal of COOL TECH's obligation to follow VET TECH's direction creates uncertainty. Article I, Section A states that COOL TECH shall manage all COOL TECH Services, which is now in conflict with VET TECH having total control over client relationships, leading to questions about the extent of VET TECH's control and COOL TECH's autonomy in service delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE I. SCOPE OF STRATEGIC ALLIANCE.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. COOL TECH shall, in a professional manner, take all steps necessary to market and produce its Mobile Generation Program and its other services (collectively the "COOL TECH Services") for clients referred to COOL TECH by VET TECH. Any engagement to perform COOL TECH Services shall be on such terms and conditions as COOL TECH may approve at its sole discretion. COOL TECH will perform, schedule, staff and manage all COOL TECH Services or with COOL TECH/VET TECH approved team. Notwithstanding the foregoing, VET TECH may, at its election, bill the client directly for COOL TECH Services and under such circumstances COOL TECH shall bill VET TECH the pre-agreed amount for the engagement as adjusted by any client-approved change orders. For any Government Sales brought by VET TECH COOL TECH agrees to be held to the same billing and payment terms as is dictated under the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) guidelines and requirements; otherwise, COOL TECH will bill the client directly. VET TECH agrees to include reference to COOL TECH in each contract and proposal involving COOL TECH Services. COOL TECH's Mobile Generation ("MG"), and other proprietary information and associated products, copyrights, trademarks, trade names and logos developed by COOL TECH shall remain the property of COOL TECH and reference to COOL TECH's rights shall be made in all uses of such materials in at least 12 point type.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes COOL TECH's obligation to respond to VET TECH's direction, while the contradicted text states COOL TECH shall 'manage all COOL TECH Services'. This creates a contradiction because VET TECH's responsibility for client relationships, without COOL TECH's required adherence to VET TECH's direction, potentially infringes on COOL TECH's management of its services, leading to uncertainty about the scope of each party's authority and contradicting the COOL TECH's autonomy in service delivery mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation, therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party's rights, titles and interests are described in the Non-Disclosure Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's rights, titles and interests are described in Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the reference from Exhibit A to Exhibit B creates a contradiction if Exhibit B does not actually define the rights, titles, and interests, or if Exhibit A does, but the contract still implies Exhibit A defines Confidential Information, as stated in Article IV. This creates an internal contradiction where the source of information doesn't line up between different sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that in the course of the performance of the VET TECH Services and the COOL TECH Services (collectively, the "Services") or additional services pursuant to this Agreement, that each may be given access to, or come into possession of, confidential information of the other party which information may contain trade secrets, proprietary data or other confidential material of that party. Therefore the parties have executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement which is attached hereto as Exhibit A, and incorporated by reference as if fully set forth herein. Materials used in any engagement undertaken pursuant to this Agreement shall not be altered or changed without the consent of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Exhibit A defines the parties' rights, titles, and interests, and the contradicted text confirms that Exhibit A is the Non-Disclosure Agreement. The changed text replaces 'Exhibit A' with 'Exhibit B'. If Exhibit B does not actually define these rights, titles, and interests (as described in the Perturbation Explanation), or if the NDA is no longer clearly referenced, it contradicts the original assertion in the contradicted text that Exhibit A (the Non-Disclosure Agreement) is incorporated by reference and governs confidential information. This creates an inconsistency in the contract regarding where these rights are defined and which agreement governs confidentiality, fitting the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement together with all documents incorporated by reference herein, constitutes the entire and sole agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, negotiations, understandings, or other matters, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire and sole agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, negotiations, understandings, or other matters, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'together with all documents incorporated by reference herein', the clause now contradicts Article I and Article IX, which explicitly refer to the Non-Disclosure Agreement attached as Exhibit A. Without the 'documents incorporated by reference' clause, the contract implies that Exhibit A is not part of the enforceable agreement, contradicting the sections that depend on it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Work performed on engagements pursuant to this Agreement by either VET TECH and/or COOL TECH and information, materials, products and deliverables developed in connection with engagements pursuant to this Agreement shall be the property of the respective parties performing the work or creating the information. All underlying methodology utilized by COOL TECH and VET TECH respectively which was created and/or developed by either prior to the date of this Agreement and utilized in the course of performing engagements pursuant to this Agreement shall not become the property of the other. Each party's rights, titles and interests are described in the Non-Disclosure Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes 'all documents incorporated by reference', ensuring Exhibit A (the Non-Disclosure Agreement) is part of the enforceable agreement. The changed text omits this phrase, creating a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which states 'Each party's rights, titles and interests are described in the Non-Disclosure Agreement attached hereto as Exhibit A'. By removing the reference to incorporated documents, the modified clause implies Exhibit A is not part of the agreement, rendering the rights and obligations detailed therein potentially unenforceable, and directly conflicting with the existing obligations within the contract, as Exhibit A is not part of the agreement, making it so that the rights, titles and interests are not applicable.
